concerns.text: T009/T010 (contract/integration tests) are placed BEFORE implementation tasks T011-T018, but tests depend on implementation artifacts to exist. Per TDD note 'Write these tests FIRST, ensure they FAIL', tests should be written after implementation exists to fail against. Current ordering creates impossible dependency: tests cannot be written before the code they test exists.

T021 and T022 are both marked [P] (parallel-safe) but both modify the same file (inference.py). T021 captures token_count, T022 captures inference_time_ms. Both require inference.py to exist and both write to the same file. Cannot run in parallel without merge conflicts.

T036-T039 (unit/contract tests) are in Phase 6 but depend on implementation tasks from Phases 3-5. T036 (simplify.py tests) depends on T012-T014; T037 (analyze.py tests) depends on T027-T034; T038 (inference.py tests) depends on T015-T016 and T021-T022. Tests cannot be written before the code they test exists.

T004 (power/sample-size analysis per FR-010) is in Phase 2 (Foundational) but FR-010 requires this in Phase 0 (Research) per plan.md. Power analysis must inform sample size BEFORE any benchmarking begins. Current placement violates research-first principle.

T012, T013, T014 all modify simplify.py but are not marked as sequential. T012 (core AST preprocessing) must complete before T013 (error handling) and T014 (semantic change detection) can be added. Consider explicit ordering or grouping.
T015 and T016 both modify inference.py but are not marked as sequential. T015 (CPU inference setup) must complete before T016 (timeout handling) can be added. Consider explicit ordering.
T027-T034 all modify analyze.py but are not marked as sequential. Each statistical test/visualization builds on previous work. Consider explicit ordering or grouping to prevent merge conflicts.
